conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
I have been really down lately. It’s been a few weeks, and I just can’t seem to shake the feeling that I should be doing better by now.
Has that feeling of being down affected your sleep at all?
Yeah, it has. I wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there thinking about all the things I could have done differently.
You mentioned thinking about things you could have done differently at night—has that feeling made it harder to get excited about things you usually enjoy during the day?
Yeah, it has. I used to love hiking, but now I just can't seem to muster the energy to go.
When you think about those things you could have done differently, what kind of feeling does that bring up for you?
It’s mostly just frustration. I feel like I should have been stronger, but I wasn't, so now I just feel stuck.
You mentioned feeling stuck—what does that feel like day to day?
It’s just... slow. Everything feels so heavy, and I just can't seem to move forward.
When you think about the future, does it feel as heavy as the day-to-day?
Yeah. I keep thinking that maybe I just need to get through this year, but the thought of being 30 and still stuck in this rut is terrifying.
With everything feeling so heavy and slow, do you find it harder to focus on things like work or reading?
Yeah, I do. I sit there and stare at the screen, and my mind just drifts off because I can’t bring myself to care about what I’m reading.
With everything feeling so heavy and your mind drifting, do you find yourself getting more easily frustrated or on edge with people?
Yeah, definitely. I get annoyed really quickly because I just can't seem to switch off that voice in my head that says I should be doing better.
You mentioned that things feel pointless - when did that start feeling this strong?
It started a few months ago, when I realized I was just going through the motions of my daily routine without actually achieving anything.
When you say you're going through the motions without achieving anything, what does a typical day look like for you now?
Just work, then coming home and staring at the TV until I go to bed. I don't really do anything anymore.
